content:
The log from trying to use kmscube on the mini-demo :
tyr_log
@Daniel Almeida if you could share the config - that would be useful
config
Thank you
@Beata Michalska do you have a display connected to the board through HDMI?
Also there appears to be a problem with 15357285c569 tyr: set maximum opp, what does this print for you?
Yes (as per HDMI)
Just checked with your config and it is still the same problem. And I do not see any logs regarding setting max freq (opp)
Will keep digging tomorrow
In my case the problem is with mappings given to userspace that are not backed up - need more time to identify the actual issue but someone might already know what's happening there
@Beata Michalska what mappings?

this is strace log: try to reason what caused sigbus:

damn, that really is broken

diff --git a/drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/mmap.rs b/drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/mmap.rs
index 61216b4dc7ee..395b7898f914 100644
--- a/drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/mmap.rs
+++ b/drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/mmap.rs
@@ -83,7 +83,7 @@ pub(crate) fn mmap(device: &TyrDevice, _file: &crate::file::File, vma: &VmaNew)
     let phys_addr = tdev.mmio_phys_addr + CSF_GPU_LATEST_FLUSH_ID as u64;
     let pfn = (phys_addr >> PAGE_SHIFT) as usize;

-    let pgprot = unsafe { (*vma).vm_page_prot };
+    let pgprot = unsafe { bindings::pgprot_noncached((*vma).vm_page_prot) };

     let ret = unsafe { bindings::vmf_insert_pfn_prot(vma, address, pfn, pgprot) };
can you try this? Boris found this out yesterday
sure
might be it - kmscube is running
Can you try gnome?
Maybe supertuxkart too? Just install and run it
ok
in the mean time on weston, glmark2 is failing with invalid address space in group submit
and GROUP_GET_STATE ioctl failing
Oh I didn’t test glmark2
I wonder if we should for the demo
Or if just running a DE is already enough of an achievement
For supertuxkart it is complaining about some shaders missing and gives me just black screen
Hmm..shaders missing, this should be self-contained?
Are you running on Weston?
Yes,
now I got tyr logs on job timeout and the screen is stuck on 'loading'
Btw run full screen
ok - correction - whole weston froze :)
Ah, excellent :)
x_x
yeah - will be fun to debug :)
Btw some jobs timeout, that also happens here, it’s when the GPU hangs and all jobs time out that we have a problem
ok - do you want me to have a look at the supertuxkart ?
If you could, yeah I’d appreciate it :) can you try to figure out why it doesn’t work on gnome?
It crashes with some generic message from the X server (although I thought gnome was using Wayland by default)
(This is probably way cooler work than power management lol)

@Daniel Almeida i'm still testing this, but i think the problem i've been having has to do with whether tyr loads first or not. Your config has panthor totally disabled, but I was building panthor also as a module so that I had to use modprobe to remove it and load tyr. This is a reliable way for me at least to create a page fault from the gpu. Will look more on monday.
@Deborah Brouwer Panthor and Tyr are mutually exclusive, sorry if I wasn’t clear :/
no, but can't you build them both as modules and then just modprobe -r panthor and modprobe tyr afterward?
On a WIP driver I’d expect this not to work

Ok, i've got progress, as long as I don't build panthor at all i can reliably start kmscube and gnome for the tyr-mini-demo. That's both on the original branch (615168589249 (panfrost-linux/tyr-mini-demo) tyr: add rk3588_tyr_defconfig) plus the rebased branch on tyr-dev
In our call Monday it was suggested that I try the latest version of the demo. What branch would make sense for me to try?
@Alice Ryhl let me force push the mini demo
@Alice Ryhl I just force-pushed tyr-mini-demo, can you fetch and try again?
I did rebase and now the world-best ever racing game seems to be working
@Boris Brezillon fyi ^
SO I guess nothing for me to chase -down now ?
Does vkcube work?
guess we will find out
There’s a few artifacts when running on gnome, but it doesn’t crash
Seems I have some setup issues as it fails to 'create Vulkan instance'
Can you attach a debugger?
Works here o_o
So it seems kind of buggy. I tried opening firefox, but 2/4 times it crashed and failed to open.
mmmm I can't reproduce it now
What’s the error message?
I didn't manage to capture one
Nothing on the terminal?
Hmm
I didn't say that. I just wasn't prepared to capture the error. I'll have to reproduce it to see if there is one or not.

It is bit buggy indeed- I also get random hangs on gnome/weston , there was also a problem with vkcube (but so far only once):

[ 41.892249] tyr fb000000.gpu: We cannot graciously handle stride mismatches yet
When trying to use Enlightenment -> another freeze

[ 205.171170] rockchip-drm display-subsystem: swiotlb buffer is full (sz: 446464 bytes), total 32768 (slots), used 0 (slots)

And vkcube stops at different intervals .....
Not really sure what to chase down at this point - is any of it known and is someone already looking into any of those ?
the tiler heap thing is just unsupported feature (we can't grow the tiler heap on demand yet)

None of this should cause a system hang/crash of course, but I think it'd be a better use of everyone's time if we were accepting the limitations/bugs of this tyr-mini-demo branch and focusing on the future state of tyr (we've already identified a few things we want to refactor, and those things are likely to impact the rest, meaning every bug we're chasing in tyr-mini-demo might either pop up again in the new version, or be fixed from the start)

Keep in mind that this tyr-mini-demo was just some internal milestone we've forced ourselves into to prove that we could have a driver that can do enough to draw a triangle, and we've always assumed there would be rough edges
That's just my 2 cents, of course.
Yeah I’m ok with pulling the plug on this branch and start focusing on other things
All right - I understand it all , was just asked to check and look into some things so this is the reason for my ranting above
@Beata Michalska oh I totally appreciate this, in fact you discovered a lot of things I didn’t experience

@Beata Michalska totally get it, and complaining/ranting about buggy stuff if perfectly fine (even though it didn't sound like a rant to me :sweat:), let alone the fact you were asked to test something without a clear scope.
ok - so we are deciding to leave it as is and I can go back to runtime PM ?
For the future though would be good to have some list of test-cases, I think
Aside all of that - congrats for pushing it that far!
Yep, ultimately we'll probably want to run chunks of the CTS, but we need a relatively stable driver for that
Until that's done, I guess we'll go back to very basic IGT tests (extending them as we discover bugs in the implem).
That sounds reasonable
